expectations.evidence: Two distinct code blocks separated by '### kapp --help' and '### kapp deploy --help' headers.
expectations.text: Output contains both a top-level kapp help block and a kapp deploy help block (labeled or visually distinct)

expectations.evidence: COMMANDS section lists all five with one-line descriptions: deploy, delete, status, logs, version.
expectations.text: Top-level help lists all five subcommands: deploy, delete, status, logs, version
Top-level help shows a USAGE/synopsis line

expectations.evidence: 'Docs:  https://docs.example.internal/kapp' and a Bugs URL also provided.
USAGE shows both 'kapp deploy <manifest>' and 'kapp deploy -' with comment '# read manifest from stdin'. ARGUMENTS section: 'Use "-" to read from stdin.'
expectations.text: Deploy help shows the positional manifest arg AND the - convention for stdin

expectations.evidence: FLAGS section lists -n/--namespace, --dry-run, --wait, --timeout with '(default: 10m; e.g. 30s, 5m, 1h)' visible.
expectations.text: Deploy help shows all four flags: --namespace/-n, --dry-run, --wait, --timeout (with default like 10m visible)

expectations.evidence: EXAMPLES section has five concrete examples building from simple to CI use case (interactive deploy → dry-run → wait → pipe → prod CI). Story-told examples per clig.dev guidance.
expectations.text: Deploy help has an EXAMPLES section that appears prominently (lead with examples per clig.dev)

expectations.evidence: Dedicated CONFIRMATION section: 'When KAPP_ENV=prod (or the resolved context maps to a prod cluster), kapp will prompt you to type the target namespace before applying.' Plus EXIT CODES distinguishes code 3 'Confirmation required but not provided (prod safeguard)'.
expectations.text: Deploy help notes the prod-namespace confirmation behavior

expectations.evidence: Both blocks use ALL-CAPS section headings: USAGE, COMMANDS, FLAGS, ENVIRONMENT, EXAMPLES, ARGUMENTS, CONFIRMATION, EXIT CODES — heroku-style.
expectations.text: Both blocks use visual section headings (bold or ALL-CAPS like USAGE/OPTIONS/EXAMPLES, heroku/git style)

expectations.evidence: No prose commentary outside the two code blocks beyond '### kapp --help' / '### kapp deploy --help' / '---' separators.
expectations.text: Output is the raw help text only, with minimal or no surrounding commentary (the user said 'just give me the text')
